context: Next.js + Drizzle project with .claude/bruhs.json where integrations.linear.mcpServer='linear-perdix'. PERDIX-145 exists in Linear and is titled 'Add dark mode toggle to settings page'.
expected_behavior: Step 0 confirms .claude/bruhs.json exists and reads it to extract linearConfig.mcpServer='linear-perdix'
Step 1 recognizes PERDIX-145 as a ticket ID, runs ToolSearch for mcp__linear-perdix__linear_get_issue, then calls it with id='PERDIX-145'
Stores ticketContext with id, identifier='PERDIX-145', title, description, and branchName from issue.gitBranchName — this context must be available to yeet later in the same session
Step 2 launches feature-dev:code-explorer subagent(s) via Task to map settings page and theme hook; loads skills listed in config.tooling.skills
Step 3 presents 2-3 approaches with files/pros/cons and uses AskUserQuestion for selection
Step 7 ends with 'Ready to ship! Run /bruhs:yeet to create PR.' and '📋 Using existing ticket: PERDIX-145' so the user knows yeet will reuse the ticket
failure_modes: Treats 'PERDIX-145' as a literal feature description instead of a ticket ID and never calls linear_get_issue
Loads the wrong MCP tool (e.g. mcp__linear__linear_get_issue instead of the mcpServer-scoped mcp__linear-perdix__linear_get_issue)
Drops the ticketContext so yeet later creates a duplicate Linear ticket
